name: Finance Analytics — 100Q
description: 100 finance analytics questions covering metrics, trends, period comparisons, forecasts, and multi-metric synthesis.

cases.name: Gross Margin Value
cases.input: What is our current gross margin?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross margin is 67.3% for Q4 FY2025, down 80bps from 68.1% in Q3.

cases.name: ARR Current Value
cases.input: What is our current annual recurring revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: ARR is $312M as of Q4 FY2025, up $25M (+8.7%) from $287M in the prior period.

cases.name: Total Revenue Q4
cases.input: What was total revenue in Q4 FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: Total revenue was $94.2M in Q4 FY2025, down $4.2M vs budget of $98.4M.

cases.name: EBITDA Definition
cases.input: How is EBITDA calculated?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA = Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization. It is net income plus interest, taxes, D&A.

cases.name: EBITDA Value Q4
cases.input: What is our EBITDA for Q4 FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA was $15.72M in Q4 FY2025, down $2.48M (-13.6%) vs Q3.

cases.name: Net Revenue Retention
cases.input: What is our net revenue retention rate?
cases.expectedOutput: NRR measures the percentage of revenue retained from existing customers including expansions minus churn and contractions.

cases.name: Cash Position
cases.input: What is our current cash position?
cases.expectedOutput: Current cash and equivalents are reported on the balance sheet as of the most recent period-end.

cases.name: Headcount Count
cases.input: How many employees do we currently have?
cases.expectedOutput: Employee headcount as of the latest period is tracked in HR systems and reported in operating metrics.

cases.name: Revenue Per Employee
cases.input: What is our revenue per employee?
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue per employee = Total Revenue / Headcount. It measures workforce productivity.

cases.name: Gross Profit Dollars
cases.input: What is gross profit in dollar terms for Q4?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross profit = Revenue - COGS. At 67.3% margin on $94.2M revenue, gross profit is approximately $63.4M.

cases.name: Operating Expenses
cases.input: What are our total operating expenses?
cases.expectedOutput: Operating expenses include headcount costs, R&D, sales & marketing, and G&A. The total is derived by subtracting EBITDA from gross profit.

cases.name: Accounts Receivable Balance
cases.input: What is the current accounts receivable balance?
cases.expectedOutput: Accounts receivable balance represents outstanding invoices billed but not yet collected, reported on the balance sheet.

cases.name: Days Sales Outstanding
cases.input: What is our DSO?
cases.expectedOutput: DSO = (AR / Revenue) × Days in Period. It measures how quickly receivables are collected.

cases.name: Bookings vs ARR
cases.input: What is the difference between bookings and ARR?
cases.expectedOutput: Bookings are the total value of new contracts signed. ARR is the annualized value of active subscriptions. Bookings convert to ARR once contracts commence.

cases.name: Magic Number
cases.input: What is the sales efficiency magic number?
cases.expectedOutput: Magic Number = Net New ARR / Sales & Marketing Spend. Values >1.0 indicate efficient growth.

cases.name: Churn Rate Definition
cases.input: How is customer churn rate calculated?
cases.expectedOutput: Churn rate = Churned ARR / Beginning-of-Period ARR. It measures the rate at which customers stop subscribing.

cases.name: COGS Components
cases.input: What is included in cost of goods sold?
cases.expectedOutput: COGS includes hosting/infrastructure, customer support, professional services delivery costs, and amortization of capitalized software.

cases.name: Rule of 40
cases.input: What is the Rule of 40?
cases.expectedOutput: Rule of 40 = Revenue Growth % + EBITDA Margin %. SaaS companies with combined score ≥40 are considered healthy.

cases.name: Deferred Revenue
cases.input: What is deferred revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: Deferred revenue (contract liabilities) represents cash received for services not yet delivered. It is a liability on the balance sheet.

cases.name: Working Capital
cases.input: How do you calculate working capital?
cases.expectedOutput: Working Capital = Current Assets - Current Liabilities. It measures short-term liquidity.

cases.name: LTV Definition
cases.input: What is customer lifetime value?
cases.expectedOutput: LTV = Average Revenue per Customer / Churn Rate (or × average contract length). It represents the total revenue expected from a customer.

cases.name: CAC Definition
cases.input: What is customer acquisition cost?
cases.expectedOutput: CAC = Total Sales & Marketing Spend / New Customers Acquired. It measures the cost to win one new customer.

cases.name: Free Cash Flow
cases.input: How is free cash flow calculated?
cases.expectedOutput: FCF = Operating Cash Flow - Capital Expenditures. It measures cash available after funding operations and capex.

cases.name: Revenue Composition
cases.input: What are the main components of our revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue comprises subscription/SaaS revenue, professional services, and support. Subscription is the largest component.

cases.name: Net Income vs EBITDA
cases.input: What is the difference between net income and EBITDA?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA excludes interest, taxes, D&A from net income. It shows operating profitability before non-cash and financing charges.

cases.name: Gross Margin Trend
cases.input: How has gross margin trended over the last 4 quarters?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross margin has compressed from 69.2% (Q1) → 68.9% (Q2) → 68.1% (Q3) → 67.3% (Q4), a 190bps decline over the year.

cases.name: ARR Growth Rate
cases.input: What is our year-over-year ARR growth rate?
cases.expectedOutput: ARR grew from $247M to $312M YoY, a 26.3% growth rate, exceeding the 25% target.

cases.name: EBITDA Margin Calculation
cases.input: What is our EBITDA margin and how does it compare to budget?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA margin is 16.7% ($15.72M / $94.2M), vs budget of 18.5%. Margin compression is 180bps unfavorable to plan.

cases.name: Revenue Mix Shift
cases.input: How has our revenue mix changed between subscription and professional services?
cases.expectedOutput: Subscription revenue has grown from 78% to 83% of total revenue over 4 quarters. PS has declined from 22% to 17% due to lower implementation demand.

cases.name: LTV/CAC Ratio
cases.input: What is our LTV:CAC ratio and what does it imply?
cases.expectedOutput: LTV:CAC of 4.2x is above the 3x minimum benchmark, indicating healthy unit economics. Payback period is approximately 14 months.

cases.name: Payback Period
cases.input: Calculate our CAC payback period.
cases.expectedOutput: CAC payback = CAC / (ACV × Gross Margin). If CAC is $42K, ACV is $36K, and GM is 72%, payback is approximately 19 months.

cases.name: Operating Leverage
cases.input: Are we showing operating leverage?
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue grew 8.7% while operating expenses grew 6.2%, showing modest operating leverage. OpEx as % of revenue declined 40bps YoY.

cases.name: Net Revenue Retention Calc
cases.input: What is our NRR this quarter and what drives it?
cases.expectedOutput: NRR is 112%, driven by 18% expansion ARR from existing accounts offset by 6% churn. Enterprise segment NRR is 121%.

cases.name: Accounts Payable Turnover
cases.input: What is our accounts payable days outstanding?
cases.expectedOutput: AP Days = (AP Balance / COGS) × 90 days. Indicates how long it takes to pay vendors on average.

cases.name: Sales Efficiency Trend
cases.input: How has our sales efficiency changed over the last year?
cases.expectedOutput: Magic Number declined from 1.4x to 0.9x over 4 quarters, driven by increasing S&M spend with slower ARR growth.

cases.name: Recurring vs Non-Recurring
cases.input: What percentage of our revenue is recurring?
cases.expectedOutput: 83% of revenue is recurring (subscription + support). 17% is non-recurring (professional services, one-time fees).

cases.name: COGS per Customer
cases.input: What is our cost of service delivery per customer?
cases.expectedOutput: COGS per customer = COGS / Customer Count. It measures the marginal cost of serving each customer.

cases.name: Quick Ratio
cases.input: What is our quick ratio?
cases.expectedOutput: Quick ratio = (Cash + AR) / Current Liabilities. It measures ability to meet short-term obligations without selling inventory.

cases.name: Burn Multiple
cases.input: What is our burn multiple?
cases.expectedOutput: Burn Multiple = Net Burn / Net New ARR. Lower is better. <1 is exceptional, 1-1.5 is good, >2 is concerning.

cases.name: Revenue per Contract
cases.input: What is our average contract value?
cases.expectedOutput: ACV = Total ARR / Number of Customers. Enterprise ACV typically differs significantly from SMB ACV.

cases.name: Deferred Revenue Movement
cases.input: How did deferred revenue change this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: Deferred revenue decreased by $2.1M this quarter, suggesting billing cadence outpaced revenue recognition, a positive indicator for future recognized revenue.

cases.name: R&D Efficiency
cases.input: What percentage of revenue is allocated to R&D?
cases.expectedOutput: R&D spend as a % of revenue is 18.4%, within the 15-20% target range for scaling SaaS companies.

cases.name: G&A Overhead
cases.input: What is our G&A as a percentage of revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: G&A is 9.1% of revenue. Mature SaaS companies typically target <8%, suggesting room for efficiency improvement.

cases.name: Gross Margin by Segment
cases.input: How does gross margin differ between our subscription and PS segments?
cases.expectedOutput: Subscription GM is 78% vs PS GM of 34%. PS dilutes blended margins. The shift to 83% subscription mix has helped overall margins despite subscription GM compression.

cases.name: Net Cash Generation
cases.input: How much cash did we generate from operations this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: Operating cash flow includes EBITDA adjusted for working capital movements, cash taxes, and interest paid.

cases.name: ARR vs Revenue Reconciliation
cases.input: How does ARR reconcile to recognized revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: ARR is the annualized run-rate of subscriptions. Recognized revenue includes ARR/4 for subscriptions plus PS completions and any one-time items.

cases.name: Customer Concentration
cases.input: What percentage of revenue comes from our top 10 customers?
cases.expectedOutput: Top 10 customers represent 28% of ARR. No single customer exceeds 5% of ARR, within healthy concentration limits.

cases.name: Pipeline Coverage
cases.input: What is our sales pipeline coverage ratio?
cases.expectedOutput: Pipeline coverage = Qualified Pipeline / Quota. 3x coverage is typically required for confident quota attainment.

cases.name: Win Rate
cases.input: What is our win rate against competitors?
cases.expectedOutput: Win rate is calculated as deals won / (deals won + deals lost to competition + deals lost to no decision).

cases.name: Headcount by Function
cases.input: How is headcount distributed across functions?
cases.expectedOutput: Engineering represents the largest function at ~40% of headcount, followed by Sales (25%), G&A (15%), CS (12%), and Marketing (8%).

cases.name: Full EBITDA Bridge
cases.input: Walk me through the full EBITDA bridge from Q3 to Q4 FY2025.
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA declined $2.48M: revenue miss (-$4.2M) partially offset by headcount savings (+$1.3M), lower T&E (+$0.4M), and reduced contractor spend (+$0.02M). Key drivers: 3 delayed enterprise renewals and Q4 restructuring savings.

cases.name: Margin Compression Root Cause
cases.input: What is driving gross margin compression over the last 4 quarters?
cases.expectedOutput: 190bps compression driven by: (1) hosting cost inflation +60bps, (2) CS headcount additions +80bps, (3) capitalized software amortization +50bps. Partially offset by automation savings -50bps. Structural headwinds from compliance requirements are unlikely to reverse near-term.

cases.name: Unit Economics Breakdown
cases.input: Analyze our unit economics for the enterprise segment.
cases.expectedOutput: Enterprise: ACV $148K, CAC $38K, Gross Margin 79%, LTV $1.24M, LTV:CAC 32.6x, Payback 11.5 months. Unit economics are strong; the constraint is sales capacity, not economics.

cases.name: FCF Reconciliation
cases.input: How does EBITDA reconcile to free cash flow?
cases.expectedOutput: FCF = EBITDA - Cash Taxes - Cash Interest - Capex ± Working Capital Changes. The gap is typically driven by capex (capitalized software), timing of customer payments (AR), and billing cadence.

cases.name: Rule of 40 Decomposition
cases.input: Calculate our Rule of 40 score and decompose it.
cases.expectedOutput: Rule of 40 = 26.3% ARR growth + 16.7% EBITDA margin = 43.0. Above 40 threshold. Growth contribution is primary driver; margin component is declining, suggesting need to improve profitability for sustained score above 40 as growth moderates.

cases.name: ARR Waterfall
cases.input: Break down our ARR movement this quarter.
cases.expectedOutput: ARR Waterfall: Opening ARR $294M + New Bookings $22M + Expansions $8M - Churn ($4M) - Contractions ($8M) = Closing ARR $312M. Net New ARR of $18M vs $28M last quarter due to lower bookings.

cases.name: Customer Cohort Analysis
cases.input: How do customer cohorts from FY2023 compare to FY2024 cohorts in terms of expansion?
cases.expectedOutput: FY2023 cohorts show 134% NRR at 24 months vs 142% for FY2024 cohorts at 24 months. Product improvements and expanded use cases are driving faster expansion in newer cohorts.

cases.name: Concentration Risk Assessment
cases.input: Assess our revenue concentration risk.
cases.expectedOutput: Risk is moderate: top customer is 4.8% of ARR; top 3 customers are 12.1%; top 10 are 28%. Geographic concentration: 72% US, 18% EMEA, 10% APAC. Vertical concentration: FinServ 34%, Tech 28%, Healthcare 18%.

cases.name: Opex Efficiency Analysis
cases.input: Identify the top 3 areas where we can improve operating expense efficiency.
cases.expectedOutput: (1) G&A at 9.1% of revenue vs 7% best-in-class — $1.8M annualized savings potential. (2) Sales efficiency decline (Magic Number 0.9x) — restructuring ROI needed. (3) COGS inflation from hosting — $0.7M annualized from cloud cost optimization.

cases.name: Churn Attribution
cases.input: What are the primary drivers of our gross revenue churn?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross churn of 6% decomposed: product-fit churn 2.4% (mostly SMB), competitive displacement 1.8%, budget cuts 1.2%, M&A/company failures 0.6%. SMB segment drives 70% of churn by count but only 38% by value.

cases.name: Payback Period Sensitivity
cases.input: How does CAC payback change if gross margin improves by 5 percentage points?
cases.expectedOutput: At current CAC $42K and ACV $36K: 72% GM → 19.4 months payback; 77% GM → 18.2 months (6% improvement). Margin gains have a multiplicative effect at higher ACVs. Priority should be on raising ACV rather than margin for maximum payback improvement.

cases.name: NRR Bridge by Segment
cases.input: Break down net revenue retention by customer segment.
cases.expectedOutput: Overall NRR 112%: Enterprise 121% (expansion 28%, churn 7%), Mid-Market 113% (expansion 18%, churn 5%), SMB 97% (expansion 8%, churn 11%). Enterprise expansion is the primary growth driver from installed base.

cases.name: Working Capital Trends
cases.input: How has working capital changed over the last year and what does it imply for cash flow?
cases.expectedOutput: Working capital improved $4.2M YoY driven by $3.1M increase in deferred revenue (positive billing trend) and $2.4M AR reduction from collections improvements. Partially offset by $1.3M increase in AP. Net neutral to slightly positive cash flow impact.

cases.name: Sales Productivity
cases.input: Analyze sales rep productivity trends over the last 4 quarters.
cases.expectedOutput: Quota attainment declined from 84% (Q1) to 71% (Q4). Average deal size grew 12% but deal velocity slowed 18%. Top quartile reps ($1.2M+ quota) maintain 97% attainment; drag is concentrated in mid-tier (40% of force, 58% attainment).

cases.name: Infrastructure Cost Efficiency
cases.input: What is our cloud infrastructure cost as a percentage of revenue and how is it trending?
cases.expectedOutput: Infrastructure is 8.4% of revenue, up from 7.8% a year ago (+60bps). Cost per customer increased $124 YoY despite a 14% increase in customer count, indicating sub-linear scaling. Targeted optimization initiative could return to 7.5% within 3 quarters.

cases.name: Steady-State Margin Analysis
cases.input: What is our estimated steady-state EBITDA margin at maturity?
cases.expectedOutput: At scale (e.g., $500M ARR), estimated steady-state margins: Gross Margin 78%, S&M 20%, R&D 14%, G&A 7% → EBITDA 37%. Current EBITDA 16.7% reflects investment phase. Path requires S&M efficiency gains (+10%) and G&A leverage (+2%) over 3-4 years.

cases.name: Scenario Analysis — Revenue Decline
cases.input: Model the EBITDA impact of a 10% revenue decline with 60% fixed costs.
cases.expectedOutput: 10% revenue decline ($94.2M → $84.8M, -$9.4M). Variable costs decline 40% of miss = -$3.8M. Fixed cost hold. EBITDA impact: -$5.6M ($15.72M → $10.1M). EBITDA margin drops from 16.7% to 11.9%. Covenant risk if debt/EBITDA threshold is at 3x.

cases.name: Gross Margin Bridge Q3 to Q4
cases.input: Build a gross margin bridge from Q3 to Q4 FY2025.
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 GM 68.1% → Q4 GM 67.3% (-80bps): hosting inflation -30bps, CS headcount additions -40bps, PS mix shift +20bps, software amortization -30bps. Net: -80bps. Trend is structural and unlikely to self-correct without pricing action.

cases.name: Cash Conversion Efficiency
cases.input: How efficiently do we convert EBITDA to free cash flow?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA-to-FCF conversion is 68%: $15.72M EBITDA → $10.7M FCF. Gap explained by capex $2.8M (capitalized dev), cash taxes $1.4M, working capital timing $0.8M. Conversion efficiency is declining (was 74% last year) due to rising capex for product development.

cases.name: Expansion Revenue Quality
cases.input: How healthy is our expansion revenue and what is driving it?
cases.expectedOutput: Expansion ARR of $8M this quarter driven by: seat expansion 42%, module upsell 35%, geographic expansion 23%. Enterprise is 78% of expansion. Net expansion rate of 18% is slightly below 20% target; main miss is upsell attachment in mid-market.

cases.name: ARR Multiple Valuation
cases.input: What ARR multiple range is implied by current comparable company valuations?
cases.expectedOutput: At 26% growth + 17% EBITDA (Rule of 40 = 43), comparable SaaS companies trade at 8-12x ARR. Applying midpoint 10x to $312M ARR implies $3.12B equity value. Discount for scale, profitability trends, and concentration risk suggests 7-9x is more defensible.

cases.name: Three-Statement Model Linkages
cases.input: How do changes in deferred revenue flow through all three financial statements?
cases.expectedOutput: New deferred revenue: Cash increases (IS neutral, CF positive). Recognition: Revenue increases (IS positive), DR decreases (BS liability). Working capital cycle: AR increases on billing, cash received reduces AR. Key ratios affected: quick ratio, current ratio, FCF conversion.

cases.name: Cohort Economics at Scale
cases.input: Model the long-run cohort economics for a $100K ACV enterprise customer acquired today.
cases.expectedOutput: 10-year LTV: Year 1 GM contribution $79K; Year 2 expansion to $115K ACV, $91K GM; Years 3-5 average $142K ACV, $112K GM. Discounted LTV at 15% WACC: $682K. CAC of $38K → 18x LTV:CAC. IRR of customer acquisition: 92% Year-1, 38% 5-year basis.

cases.name: Capital Efficiency Score
cases.input: Compute and benchmark our capital efficiency score.
cases.expectedOutput: Capital Efficiency = Total ARR / Total Capital Raised. $312M ARR / $180M raised = 1.73x. Benchmark: top quartile SaaS is >2x at our stage; median is 1.2x. We're above median but below top quartile. Improvement path: reduce S&M spend while maintaining NRR-driven growth.

cases.name: Operating Leverage Sensitivity
cases.input: At what revenue run rate do we achieve 25% EBITDA margin assuming current cost structure?
cases.expectedOutput: Fixed cost base ~$62M annually. Variable cost ratio ~40% of revenue. At 25% EBITDA margin: Revenue × (1 - 0.40 - EBITDA%) = Fixed → R × 0.35 = $62M → R = $177M quarterly ($708M ARR run-rate). Requires 127% growth from current. Timeline: ~4 years at 26% CAGR.

cases.name: Margin Expansion Roadmap
cases.input: Build a 3-year margin expansion roadmap to reach 25% EBITDA.
cases.expectedOutput: FY25 16.7% → FY26 19.2% (+250bps from S&M efficiency +150bps, G&A leverage +100bps) → FY27 21.8% (+260bps from gross margin recovery +80bps, R&D scale +100bps, S&M +80bps) → FY28 25.1% (+330bps from continued scale). Key risk: growth rate slowdown.

cases.name: Dual-Track Scenario
cases.input: Compare financial outcomes under a growth-first vs profitability-first strategy over 3 years.
cases.expectedOutput: Growth-first: 30% CAGR, EBITDA 12-16%, Rule of 40 ≈42-46, ARR $688M FY28. Profitability-first: 18% CAGR, EBITDA 22-26%, Rule of 40 ≈40-44, ARR $488M FY28. Value creation similar at 8-10x ARR multiple. Growth-first requires $80M additional capital; profitability-first is FCF-funded by FY27.

cases.name: TAM Penetration Analysis
cases.input: Estimate our total addressable market penetration.
cases.expectedOutput: Estimated TAM $28B (global mid-market to enterprise finance software). Current ARR $312M = 1.1% penetration. SAM (serviceable: English-speaking, 100-5000 employee firms) = $8.4B → 3.7% penetration. Growth to $1B ARR = 11.9% SAM penetration, historically achievable for category leaders.

cases.name: Structural vs Cyclical Revenue
cases.input: Decompose our revenue miss into structural vs cyclical components.
cases.expectedOutput: $4.2M total miss: Cyclical $2.8M (enterprise procurement freeze, macro spending caution — expected to reverse) + Structural $1.4M (PS secular decline as product becomes self-serve, product gaps in mid-market module). Recommendation: address structural component via product roadmap; cyclical will self-correct Q1.

cases.name: Normalized EBITDA
cases.input: What is normalized EBITDA after adjusting for one-time items?
cases.expectedOutput: Reported EBITDA $15.72M. Add-backs: severance $0.8M, restructuring advisory fees $0.4M, accelerated lease termination $0.3M, legal settlement $0.2M. Adjusted EBITDA $17.42M (18.5% margin). Adjusted metric is in-line with budget; reported miss is primarily driven by one-time charges.

cases.name: Revenue Growth Decomposition
cases.input: Decompose ARR growth into its components: new logos, expansion, churn.
cases.expectedOutput: Net New ARR $18M: New Logo ARR $14M + Expansion ARR $8M - Gross Churn ($4M) = $18M. New logo engine slowing (was $22M last quarter). Expansion performing (8% quarterly expansion rate). Gross churn in line ($4M, 5.4% annualized gross churn rate).

cases.name: Incremental Margin Analysis
cases.input: What is our incremental EBITDA margin on the next $10M of revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: Incremental margin = 1 - incremental variable cost rate. At current scale, each $1 of new subscription revenue carries ~78% GM. After incremental S&M (20% of new ARR) and minimal R&D/G&A uplift, incremental EBITDA margin is approximately 48-52%. This creates strong operating leverage at scale.

cases.name: IPO Readiness Metrics
cases.input: Assess our readiness for a public market listing based on key metrics.
cases.expectedOutput: Positive indicators: $312M ARR (+26% YoY), 112% NRR, Rule of 40 = 43, positive FCF. Concerns: declining sales efficiency (Magic Number 0.9x), gross margin compression (-190bps), G&A not yet scaled. Recommendation: 12-18 months of operational improvement before IPO to demonstrate trajectory on S&M efficiency and gross margin stabilization.

cases.name: Pricing Power Analysis
cases.input: How much pricing power do we have and what does the data show?
cases.expectedOutput: Logo churn attributable to pricing is <1% (vs 2.4% product-fit churn). Last 3 price increases (avg 7%) showed <4% incremental churn. NPS among paying customers is 42. Conclusion: meaningful pricing power exists; annual 5-7% price increases are defensible without material churn impact.

cases.name: Segment Profitability
cases.input: Which customer segment is most profitable on a contribution margin basis?
cases.expectedOutput: Enterprise: 79% GM, 14% allocated S&M, CM 65%; Mid-Market: 76% GM, 18% S&M, CM 58%; SMB: 71% GM, 32% S&M (high touch), CM 39%. Enterprise is most profitable but SMB has highest volume. Recommended reallocation: reduce high-touch SMB to 20% S&M via self-serve motion to bring SMB CM to 51%.

cases.name: Debt Capacity Analysis
cases.input: How much debt could we service given our current EBITDA?
cases.expectedOutput: At $15.72M quarterly EBITDA ($62.9M annualized), conservative 3x leverage = $188M debt capacity. At 8% interest rate, annual interest = $15M (24% of EBITDA) — tight but serviceable. Covenant risk: need EBITDA to sustain >$55M annually to maintain 3x ratio at $165M. Not recommended at current scale.

cases.name: M&A Synergy Analysis
cases.input: Model the synergies from acquiring a $20M ARR competitor at 8x ARR.
cases.expectedOutput: Acquisition cost $160M. Revenue synergies: cross-sell $3M ARR ($12M over 4yr), go-to-market leverage $1.5M ARR. Cost synergies: G&A overlap $2.2M annual, COGS scale $0.8M. Payback: 6.2 years on total synergies. IRR: 14% at 10x blended exit multiple. Marginal deal — only attractive if competitive/defensive rationale.

cases.name: Geographic Expansion ROI
cases.input: Model the ROI of expanding into the APAC market.
cases.expectedOutput: APAC investment: $4.2M Year 1 (2 regional hires, infrastructure, compliance). TAM $2.8B serviceable. At 0.5% capture over 4 years = $14M ARR. LTV:CAC 3.1x (lower due to compliance overhead, longer sales cycles). Breakeven: Month 28. Risk-adjusted IRR: 22%. Proceed with phased entry starting with Singapore/ANZ.

cases.name: Technology Debt Quantification
cases.input: How do you quantify the financial cost of technical debt?
cases.expectedOutput: Technical debt cost = (Additional maintenance hours × hourly rate) + (feature velocity reduction × opportunity cost per feature) + (increased COGS from inefficiency). Estimated annual cost: $2.4M in eng capacity + $1.8M in deferred revenue from slower delivery = $4.2M total. Represents 4.5% of revenue.

cases.name: PLG vs SLG Economics
cases.input: Compare the unit economics of our product-led vs sales-led acquisition motions.
cases.expectedOutput: PLG (free-to-paid): CAC $4.2K, ACV $14K, GM 82%, Payback 4.1 months, NRR 108%. SLG (enterprise): CAC $38K, ACV $148K, GM 79%, Payback 11.4 months, NRR 121%. SLG generates 10x more LTV per acquisition. Optimal mix: PLG as top-of-funnel, SLG for expansion. Resource allocation: 70% SLG, 30% PLG investment.

cases.name: Forecast Accuracy
cases.input: How do we measure forecast accuracy?
cases.expectedOutput: Forecast accuracy = 1 - |Actual - Forecast| / Forecast. MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error) is the standard metric. ±5% is excellent; ±10% is acceptable for quarterly forecasts.

cases.name: Budget vs Actual
cases.input: What is the difference between budget and actual revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: Budget was $98.4M; actual was $94.2M. Unfavorable variance of $4.2M or -4.3%.

cases.name: Net Margin
cases.input: What is our net profit margin?
cases.expectedOutput: Net margin = Net Income / Revenue. After interest, taxes, D&A, net margin is typically significantly below EBITDA margin.

cases.name: Cash Runway
cases.input: What is our current cash runway?
cases.expectedOutput: Cash runway = Current Cash / Monthly Burn Rate. If cash-flow positive, runway is effectively infinite; if burning, divide cash balance by net monthly outflow.

cases.name: Annualized Run Rate
cases.input: What is the difference between ARR and annualized run rate?
cases.expectedOutput: ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) is the annualized value of active subscription contracts. Annualized run rate is quarterly revenue × 4, which includes all revenue types including non-recurring.

cases.name: Churn vs Contraction
cases.input: What is the difference between churn and contraction?
cases.expectedOutput: Churn is complete cancellation of a subscription (customer leaves). Contraction is a reduction in ARR from an existing customer who stays but downgrades or removes seats.

cases.name: Revenue Seasonality
cases.input: How much seasonality is there in our bookings?
cases.expectedOutput: Q4 is strongest bookings quarter (typically 35-40% of annual bookings) due to year-end budget cycles. Q1 is softest. Seasonal adjustment needed when comparing QoQ performance.

cases.name: Blended vs Marginal CAC
cases.input: What is the difference between blended and marginal CAC and why does it matter?
cases.expectedOutput: Blended CAC averages across all acquisition channels. Marginal CAC is the cost of the next incremental customer. As you saturate efficient channels, marginal CAC rises faster than blended CAC. This distinction matters for S&M investment decisions: the marginal channel ROI determines additional investment justification.

cases.name: Revenue Quality Score
cases.input: Assess the quality of our revenue.
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue quality scorecard: 83% recurring (+), no single customer >5% (+), 112% NRR (+), 28% geographic concentration in North America (neutral), 18% PS mix (mixed — lower margin, lumpy), 4% contract-level concentration in MSAs >$500K (minor risk). Overall: High-quality revenue with manageable concentration.

cases.name: Long-Run Financial Model
cases.input: Build a summary long-run financial model from current to $1B ARR.
cases.expectedOutput: At $1B ARR (est. FY2031 at ~21% CAGR from $312M): Gross Margin 80% ($800M), S&M 18% ($180M), R&D 12% ($120M), G&A 6% ($60M) → EBITDA 44% ($440M). FCF conversion ~90%. Rule of 40 = 21% growth + 44% EBITDA = 65. EV at 10x ARR = $10B. Requires 4-5 years of disciplined execution.
